title: Yacht Rental Across the US | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Yacht Rental through BoatHire24 — book luxury yachts, sportfishers and sailing yachts around the US directly from owners and operators. Transparent pricing
intro: Yacht Rental through BoatHire24 — book luxury yachts, sportfishers and sailing yachts around the US directly from owners and operators. Transparent pricing, instant confirmation, no renter service fees. This page covers the fleet, typical rates, and how to list your own boat if you keep one nearby.
bodyHtml:
What's available around the US

Demand centres on luxury yachts, sportfishers and sailing yachts. Typical inventory: Azimut, Sunseeker, Princess motor yachts.

  • Half-day (4h), full-day (8h), and sunset cruise (2h) rentals
  • Captain-included and self-drive options
  • Group sizes 4–14 typical; party platforms up to 18

Day rate ranges (2026)

BoatHalf-dayFull day
Pontoon 22–26 ft$350–$650$550–$1,000
Tritoon party (10–14)$500–$850$800–$1,300
Ski / wakeboard$450–$750$750–$1,200
Sport yacht 30–40 ft$1,200–$2,200$2,000–$3,800

Captain $180–$280/day. Fuel pass-through. Cleaning $40–$80.

Best time of year

Peak Memorial Day to Labor Day. Shoulder weekends 20–30% cheaper. Holiday weekends fill 4–8 weeks ahead.

Own a boat on the US? List it

List free on BoatHire24, flat 15% commission. See vs Boatsetter and vs GetMyBoat.

Nearby

  • Boat Rental Miami Beach
  • Boat Rental Fort Lauderdale Marina
  • Boat Rental San Diego Bay

faqs.q: How much does it cost to rent a boat at the US?
faqs.a: Half-day $350–$2,200 depending on boat type; full-day 50–80% more. Captain extra. Most groups spend $600–$1,400 for a half-day.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence?
faqs.a: Most states require a Boating Safety Course Certificate for self-drive operators born after a state cutoff. Skipper-included bypasses it.

faqs.q: BYO food and drinks?
faqs.a: Yes — almost always. Glass restrictions on some lakes.

faqs.q: Weather backup?
faqs.a: Operator-cancelled for weather = full refund or free reschedule.

faqs.q: Pontoon capacity?
faqs.a: 22–24ft: 8–10 guests; 26–28ft: 12–14; party platforms 16+. Coast Guard placard at the helm.

faqs.q: How early to book?
faqs.a: Peak summer weekends: 4–8 weeks. Holidays: 8–12 weeks. Shoulder: a few days.

faqs.q: Sunset cruise at the US?
faqs.a: Most listings offer a 2h sunset option. Common on pontoons and sport yachts.

bodyHtml:

Cadre : lac alpin. Bateaux : bateaux électriques, à moteur. Spots : eaux turquoise des Alpes.

Tarifs 2026

TypeDemi-journéeJournée
Sans permis (≤6 CV)€140–€220€220–€340
Semi-rigide 6–8m€350–€550€550–€850
Bateau moteur 8–10m€600–€900€900–€1,400
Voilier 35–45 ft€450–€750€750–€1,300

Skipper €180–€280/jour. Carburant à part.

Vous avez un bateau ? Listez-le

Listing gratuit, commission 15% flat. Voir vs Click&Boat.

À proximité

  • Location Bateau Annecy Lac
  • Location Bateau Lac Du Bourget

faqs.q: Combien coûte la location à France ?
faqs.a: De €140 demi-journée sans permis à €1,400 journée bateau moteur.

faqs.q: Permis bateau requis ?
faqs.a: Non pour ≤ 6 CV. Permis côtier pour bateaux plus puissants.

faqs.q: BYO ?
faqs.a: Oui presque toujours.

faqs.q: Météo ?
faqs.a: Annulation côté loueur = remboursement intégral ou report.

faqs.q: Capacité ?
faqs.a: Sans permis 4–6 personnes ; semi-rigide 8–10 ; bateau moteur 10–12.

faqs.q: Réserver à l'avance ?
faqs.a: Août : 4–6 semaines. Juillet : 2–3. Mai/Juin/Sept : quelques jours.

faqs.q: Annulation ?

intro: Pedal Boat Rental through BoatHire24 — book day boats and ski boats around the US directly from owners and operators. Transparent pricing, instant confirmation, no renter service fees. This page covers the fleet, typical rates, and how to list your own boat if you keep one nearby.
bodyHtml:
What's available around the US

Demand centres on day boats and ski boats. Typical inventory: Bayliner deck boats, ski boats, fishing pontoons.

  • Half-day (4h), full-day (8h), and sunset cruise (2h) rentals
  • Captain-included and self-drive options
  • Group sizes 4–14 typical; party platforms up to 18

Day rate ranges (2026)

BoatHalf-dayFull day
Pontoon 22–26 ft$350–$650$550–$1,000
Tritoon party (10–14)$500–$850$800–$1,300
Ski / wakeboard$450–$750$750–$1,200
Sport yacht 30–40 ft$1,200–$2,200$2,000–$3,800

Captain $180–$280/day. Fuel pass-through. Cleaning $40–$80.

Best time of year

Peak Memorial Day to Labor Day. Shoulder weekends 20–30% cheaper. Holiday weekends fill 4–8 weeks ahead.

Own a boat on the US? List it

List free on BoatHire24, flat 15% commission. See vs Boatsetter and vs GetMyBoat.

Nearby

  • Electric Boat Rental Usa
  • Big Bear Boat Rentals

faqs.q: How much does it cost to rent a boat at the US?
faqs.a: Half-day $350–$2,200 depending on boat type; full-day 50–80% more. Captain extra. Most groups spend $600–$1,400 for a half-day.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence?
faqs.a: Most states require a Boating Safety Course Certificate for self-drive operators born after a state cutoff. Skipper-included bypasses it.

faqs.q: BYO food and drinks?
faqs.a: Yes — almost always. Glass restrictions on some lakes.

faqs.q: Weather backup?
faqs.a: Operator-cancelled for weather = full refund or free reschedule.

faqs.q: Pontoon capacity?
faqs.a: 22–24ft: 8–10 guests; 26–28ft: 12–14; party platforms 16+. Coast Guard placard at the helm.

faqs.q: How early to book?
faqs.a: Peak summer weekends: 4–8 weeks. Holidays: 8–12 weeks. Shoulder: a few days.

faqs.q: Sunset cruise at the US?
faqs.a: Most listings offer a 2h sunset option. Common on pontoons and sport yachts.

title: Airbnb for Boats — The Owner's Guide to P2P Boat Rental Platforms | BoatHire24
h1: Airbnb for Boats: The Owner's Guide
metaDescription: Airbnb for boats explained — how peer-to-peer boat rental platforms work for owners, what to expect on commission, insurance, and how BoatHire24 compares.
intro: 'Airbnb for boats' is the shorthand for peer-to-peer boat rental platforms — Boatsetter, Click&Boat, Samboat, GetMyBoat, Borrow A Boat, and BoatHire24. Same architecture as Airbnb for accommodation: private owners list their boats, renters book directly, the platform handles payments and screening. This page explains the model from an owner's angle: what you'll actually earn, how the platforms differ, where each is strongest.
bodyHtml:
How the Airbnb-for-boats model works

The owner lists their boat with photos, description, calendar and price. A renter searches the platform (often by location + dates), books a date, pays through the platform's checkout. The owner approves (or rejects) the booking. On the day, owner and renter meet at the marina for handover, run the charter, return the boat. Platform releases the payout to the owner net of commission. Same cycle, repeated 8–60 times a year per boat depending on listing quality.

Which 'Airbnb for boats' platform should you list on?

PlatformOwner commissionRenter feeStrongest market
BoatHire2415%0%Mediterranean Europe
Boatsetter~25–35% effective10–15%US — Florida, California, Texas
Click&Boat15–22%~6%France, EU Mediterranean
Samboat15–22%~6%Same as Click&Boat (same parent)
GetMyBoat7–10%7–10%US lakes, Australia, Caribbean
Borrow A Boat~15–20%~5–10%UK

Most successful owners list on 2 platforms with iCal calendar sync. Single-platform owners leave 25–40% of potential bookings on the table.

What you can realistically earn

Heavily depends on the boat, the market, and the listing quality. Indicative net (after platform commission, fuel pass-through, cleaning, insurance):

  • Small motor boat (8 m, no licence required): $4,000–$12,000/season
  • 10–12 m motor yacht: $20,000–$45,000/season
  • 14–18 m yacht (crewed): $50,000–€120,000/season
  • Sailing yacht 12–15 m: $12,000–$30,000/season
  • Catamaran 40–45 ft: $35,000–$80,000/season
  • Jet ski (per machine): $7,000–$16,000/season

'Season' means roughly May–October in the Mediterranean and Northern Europe; year-round in Florida, California, parts of Australia. Top quartile of owners (excellent listings + reviews + multi-platform) clear 30–50% more than these midpoints.

Insurance — the part that's not like Airbnb

Airbnb-for-homes bundles host insurance globally. Boat platforms can't — marine insurance is regional, regulated separately by each country, and pleasure-use policies explicitly exclude commercial charter. As an owner you need an annual charter insurance policy regardless of which platform you list on. Boatsetter bundles its own US-flagged policy via Geico Marine; Click&Boat and BoatHire24 connect you with an independent broker.

Get started

Become a host on BoatHire24 — free listing, flat 15% commission, calendar control. See also: comparison pages above.

faqs.q: What is "Airbnb for boats"?
faqs.a: Peer-to-peer boat rental platforms operating the same business model as Airbnb but in the marine vertical. Boatsetter, Click&Boat, Samboat, GetMyBoat, Borrow A Boat, and BoatHire24 are the major operators. Owners list their boats; renters book directly; the platform handles payments, screening, and dispute resolution.

faqs.q: Does Airbnb itself rent boats?
faqs.a: No. Airbnb does not operate boat rentals directly. The 'Airbnb for boats' phrase describes the marketplace model in the marine vertical, not Airbnb's actual product. Airbnb has experimented with boat-stay listings in some markets but doesn't run a boat rental marketplace.

faqs.q: Which platform pays out best to owners?
faqs.a: Depends on geography. In the Mediterranean, BoatHire24's flat 15% beats Click&Boat's 15–22% and Boatsetter's effective 25–35% after insurance. In US lake markets, GetMyBoat's 7–10% looks cheapest but cancellation rates erode that advantage. Most owners list on 2+ platforms with iCal sync.

faqs.q: Can I list on multiple Airbnb-for-boats platforms?
faqs.a: Yes. Almost all owners cross-list. iCal calendar sync prevents double-booking. Most platforms allow it explicitly. Cross-listing typically lifts bookings 25–40% in Year 1 versus single-platform because audience overlap is low.

faqs.q: Do I need insurance to list?
faqs.a: Yes. Pleasure-use boat policies in every major market exclude commercial charter activity. You need an annual charter-grade policy. Boatsetter US offers a bundled option; other platforms refer you to independent brokers (Pantaenius in the Med, BoatUS in the US, GJW Direct in the UK).

faqs.q: How much commission do these platforms charge?
faqs.a: Owner-side commission ranges from 7% (GetMyBoat) to 35% effective (Boatsetter after insurance load). Most platforms charge 15–22%. Renter-side service fees range from 0% (BoatHire24) to 15% (Boatsetter). Net to owner depends on both.

faqs.q: Is "Airbnb for boats" actually growing?
faqs.a: Yes — the global peer-to-peer boat rental market grew roughly 4× from 2019 to 2025 and continues to outpace traditional charter brokers. Pandemic-era growth was steep; post-pandemic, growth has stabilised around 15–25% annually.

title: Boat Rental Insurance for Owners (US) — Cover, Costs, Brokers | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Boat rental insurance for US owners: charter coverage, $1M liability minimums, premiums by boat size, brokers to contact. P2P insurance explained.
intro: Boat rental insurance for owners in the US works differently from the Spanish-market explainer we publish — different insurers, different regulatory base, different bundled-policy options through platforms. This page covers the US side: what cover you need to list a boat for paid charter, what premiums run in 2026, the brokers and platforms that offer it, and how it compares to standalone EU charter insurance covered on our Spain page.
bodyHtml:
What pleasure-use policies don't cover

Every major US recreational marine insurer — Progressive, Allstate, GEICO Marine, Markel, Travelers — excludes paid charter activity from standard pleasure-use policies. The exclusion language varies but the effect is the same: one paid rental voids the policy for that incident and often for the year. Check the 'Exclusions' section of your existing policy for terms like 'charter use', 'rented out', 'commercial use' or 'paid passengers'.

What charter coverage looks like

  • $1M passenger liability minimum. US regulatory standard for paid passenger transport. Most insurers underwrite to $1M–$2M.
  • Hull cover at agreed value with charter use disclosed on the schedule.
  • $300K pollution liability (Oil Pollution Act baseline; some marinas require $500K).
  • Crew coverage if you nominate captain or mate.
  • Theft and vandalism riders for marine electronics, dinghy, outboard.

Premium ranges — US, 2026

BoatHull valueAnnual charter premium
22 ft pontoon$30k–$60k$900–$1,400
26 ft tritoon party boat$55k–$90k$1,200–$1,900
32 ft sport yacht$150k–$280k$2,200–$3,800
42 ft motor yacht$400k–$700k$3,500–$5,500
50 ft+ motor yacht$800k+$5,500–$9,000+
30 ft sailing yacht$60k–$120k$1,400–$2,400
40 ft catamaran$250k–$450k$2,800–$4,500
Sea-Doo / Yamaha jet ski$15k–$25k$700–$1,100

Surcharges: 15–35% for owners without prior charter experience, hulls older than 20 years without recent refit, marina mooring on swing rather than slip, claims history.

Brokers and bundled platform options

  • Boatsetter (GEICO Marine / BoatUS). Per-booking bundled policy with $1M liability, available only on Boatsetter listings. Convenient for US owners listing primarily there. See Boatsetter alternative comparison.
  • BoatUS Direct. Standalone charter policy. Works for any platform. Strong claims service. Quote online.
  • Markel Marine. Underwrites GetMyBoat's bundled option and offers standalone charter cover.
  • Allianz / AGCS Marine. Larger yachts (40 ft+) with international cruising — best for owners who run Caribbean charters from Florida.
  • SkiSafe (water-sports specific). Wakeboard and ski boat charter — niche but strong on this category.

Peer-to-peer boat rental insurance

This phrase ($13.75 CPC — high-value owner search) usually refers to the per-booking bundled policies Boatsetter and GetMyBoat offer. Both activate when a renter pays, lapse when the boat returns. Convenient but more expensive over a season than standalone annual cover. Standalone wins for owners doing 12+ charters a year; bundled wins for owners doing fewer than 6.

Get started — list with charter insurance ready

List your boat on BoatHire24. We connect you to the right broker for your boat, geography, and charter frequency. EU owners: see our Spain-specific page for Pantaenius / Una / Mapfre options.

faqs.q: Does my normal boat insurance cover renting it out?
faqs.a: No. Every major US recreational marine insurer (Progressive, Allstate, GEICO Marine pleasure, Markel, Travelers) explicitly excludes paid charter activity. One paid rental can void the policy for that incident and often for the policy year. You need a separate charter-grade policy.

faqs.q: How much does boat rental insurance cost?
faqs.a: $900–$1,400/year for a 22 ft pontoon; $1,200–$1,900 for a 26 ft party boat; $2,200–$3,800 for a 32 ft sport yacht; $3,500–$5,500 for a 42 ft motor yacht; $700–$1,100 for a jet ski. Premiums depend on hull value, claims history, charter frequency, and mooring type.

faqs.q: What is peer-to-peer boat rental insurance?
faqs.a: Per-booking bundled coverage Boatsetter and GetMyBoat activate during paid charters, with $1M liability and hull cover. Convenient but more expensive than standalone annual cover for owners doing more than 12 charters a year.

faqs.q: Do I need insurance to list on Boatsetter / GetMyBoat / Click&Boat?
faqs.a: Boatsetter's bundled policy covers your boat during paid charters on their platform but doesn't replace owner-held annual cover. Click&Boat treats insurance as add-on. GetMyBoat does too. Standalone annual charter insurance is the safe baseline.

faqs.q: How much liability cover do I need?
faqs.a: US regulatory standard for paid passenger transport: $1M. Many state marinas now require $500K minimum pollution liability and $1M hull liability for slip leases. Recommended underwriting: $1M passenger liability, $1M hull liability, $300K–$500K pollution.

faqs.q: Who are the best brokers for US owners?
faqs.a: BoatUS Direct (strong claims), Markel Marine (broad coverage), Allianz/AGCS Marine (larger yachts and international), SkiSafe (water-sports specific). Boatsetter's bundled GEICO/BoatUS option works only on their platform.

faqs.q: What about EU-flagged boats?
faqs.a: Different market. EU regulators require local cover from local-market underwriters (Pantaenius, Una, Mapfre Náutica). See our Spain insurance page. US insurers generally won't underwrite EU-flagged vessels.

kw: how much can i earn renting my boat
title: How Much Can I Earn Renting My Boat? Realistic 2026 Numbers | BoatHire24
h1: How Much Can I Earn Renting My Boat?
metaDescription: Realistic 2026 earnings from renting your boat: $8,000–$120,000+/season by boat type. Real numbers from BoatHire24 owner data — no marketing fluff.
intro: The honest answer to how much can I earn renting my boat depends on five things: boat type and size, location (Mediterranean / Florida / lake / cold-water), listing quality (photos and copy), pricing (most owners under-price), and whether you list on 1 or 2+ platforms. This page works through the math by boat category, with 2026 actual numbers from owner statements on our platform.
bodyHtml:
The honest by-boat-type earnings table

Boat typeCharter days/seasonGross revenueNet to owner (after platform, fuel, cleaning, insurance)
License-free 5m RIB / 22ft pontoon14–32$5,000–$14,000$3,500–$10,000
26ft party pontoon / 8m motor boat18–34$10,000–$22,000$7,000–$16,000
10–12m motor yacht / 32ft sport yacht22–42$25,000–$55,000$18,000–$40,000
14–18m yacht28–48$55,000–$120,000$38,000–$85,000
22m+ crewed yacht26–40$120,000–$250,000$80,000–$170,000
10–14m sailing yacht18–32$15,000–$36,000$10,000–$26,000
40–45ft catamaran28–40$45,000–$95,000$32,000–$70,000
Jet ski (per machine)120–180 hours$11,000–$22,000$7,500–$16,000

Wide ranges because listing quality, season-length and pricing matter more than the boat. Mid-quartile is around the middle of each range; top quartile (excellent photos, video, multi-platform listing, dynamic pricing) clears 25–50% above the midpoint.

What drives the gap between bottom and top quartile

  1. Photos. Phone shots taken in flat midday light vs golden-hour pro photos: 2–3× difference in click-through and conversion.
  2. Video. A 60-second on-board video doubles enquiry rate on every platform.
  3. Pricing strategy. Fixed-rate owners under-earn vs dynamic owners. Peak-weekend uplift of 25–40% is normal in Mediterranean / Florida markets.
  4. Calendar openness. Owners who block 40% of dates earn 40% less. Common error: blocking 'just in case I want to use it' weekends.
  5. Multi-platform listing. Cross-listing with iCal sync lifts bookings 25–40%.
  6. Response time. Owners who reply to enquiries within 1 hour convert 2× owners who reply after 4+ hours.

Costs to subtract

Line itemTypical % of gross
Platform commission7–22%
Insurance (annualised)5–10%
Captain (if charter requires)10–25%
Fuel pass-through (often renter pays)0–5% net
Cleaning per charter3–6%
Increased maintenance vs pleasure use10–20%
Marina (commercial uplift)10–25% above pleasure rate

Net to owner typically lands at 55–75% of gross for skippered/managed charter and 65–80% for owner-operated.

The one-line summary

A well-photographed 10–12 m motor yacht in a strong charter market earns its owner roughly $20,000–$40,000 net per season after all costs. Smaller boats earn less; larger crewed yachts earn substantially more; the gap between a great listing and a bad listing is bigger than the gap between two boats of similar size.

Get started

List your boat on BoatHire24. Compare platforms: vs Boatsetter, vs Click&Boat, vs GetMyBoat.

faqs.q: How much money can I make renting out my boat?
faqs.a: Realistic net to owner: $3,500–$10,000/season for a small license-free boat or 22ft pontoon; $18,000–$40,000 for a 10–12m motor yacht; $38,000–$85,000 for a 14–18m yacht; $80,000–$170,000 for a 22m+ crewed yacht. Big variance because listing quality drives the result more than the boat.

faqs.q: Is renting my boat actually profitable?
faqs.a: Mostly yes if the boat is in a strong charter market (Mediterranean coast, Florida, US/UK lakes, Sydney Harbour) and the listing is well-built (pro photos, video, multi-platform). Marginal if the boat is in a slow market or the listing is poor. Owners doing fewer than 12 charters a year often net less than annual operating costs.

faqs.q: What boat size makes the most money?
faqs.a: Absolute earnings: 18–24m crewed yachts net $60–$170K/season. Return on capital: 10–14m motor yachts and 40–45ft catamarans net 6–10% of hull value annually. Return on time-spent: jet skis (high revenue per maintenance hour). License-free boats earn least absolute but best per-dollar-invested.

faqs.q: How many charter days can I realistically expect?
faqs.a: 12–18 charter days in the bottom quartile; 28–40 in the mid-range; 45–60+ in the top quartile. Peak markets (Marbella, Cannes, Miami, Sydney Harbour) skew higher; quieter markets (smaller US lakes, off-season Mediterranean) skew lower.

faqs.q: Does season length matter?
faqs.a: Yes, hugely. Mediterranean + cold-climate North America = 5–6 month effective charter season (May–October). Florida + Australia + tropical = 9–12 month season. The same boat in Miami earns roughly 2× what it earns in Boston due to season length alone.

faqs.q: What ROI on the boat purchase is realistic?
faqs.a: Gross: 6–11% on a 10–14m motor yacht in a strong market. Net (after all costs and reasonable maintenance): 4–7%. Charter income alone won't typically pay off a financed purchase, but it covers operating costs (marina, insurance, maintenance) and leaves a surplus.

faqs.q: How long until I break even?
faqs.a: Operating break-even (charter covers slip + insurance + maintenance): Year 1 for most well-listed boats. Recovery of purchase price: depends on financing. Owners buying outright typically see 12–18 year recovery via charter alone; those financing rarely break even on financing through charter — but operating cost coverage is the real win.

title: Boat Charter Management for Owners — Full-Service vs DIY | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Boat charter management explained: full-service vs DIY listing. Commission, handover, insurance, calendar — what owners should expect in 2026.
intro: Boat charter management is the full-service end of peer-to-peer boat rental: the platform doesn't just list your boat — it handles renter screening, in-person check-in/out, paperwork (despacho in Spain, despacho equivalent elsewhere), damage triage, maintenance coordination. This page covers what charter management actually costs vs DIY listing, where it makes sense, where you're paying for service you don't need.
bodyHtml:
Full-service charter management — what's included

  • Listing creation: pro photos, video, copy in 2+ languages.
  • Calendar and pricing management (often dynamic).
  • Renter screening: voice or video call, group composition check, prior history.
  • Paperwork: charter permits (despacho in Spain, equivalents elsewhere).
  • In-person handover at the slip — safety briefing, key handover, photo check-in.
  • Renter day support — WhatsApp on-call during charter.
  • Return inspection and damage triage within 60 minutes.
  • Deposit release / capture and dispute handling.
  • Owner statements and payout (weekly / monthly).
  • Cleaning coordination.
  • Insurance broker referral and claims liaison.

Typical full-service commission: 18–25% of gross.

DIY listing — what you do yourself

  • Write your own listing copy, take phone photos.
  • Manage calendar manually.
  • Respond to renter enquiries.
  • Show up at the slip for handover.
  • Run renter screening (or accept platform's basic screening).
  • Inspect on return, dispute damage if needed.
  • File your own charter permits.
  • Manage your own insurance.

Typical platform-only commission: 7–15% (BoatHire24: 15%; GetMyBoat: 7–10%).

Cost comparison — €40,000-gross yacht

ApproachCommissionOwner time / seasonNet to owner
BoatHire24 self-listed15% = €6,00050–80 hrs€34,000 minus your costs
BoatHire24 full-service18–22% = €7,200–€8,800~10 hrs€31,200–€32,800
Local charter manager (third-party)22–35% = €8,800–€14,000~5 hrs€26,000–€31,200

Full-service charges €1,800–€3,000 more in absolute commission, in exchange for ~70 hours of your time. Worth it for owners who live overseas, work full-time, or own multiple boats.

When charter management makes sense

  1. Owner lives in a different country from the boat.
  2. Owner has full-time job and can't be on-call at the marina.
  3. Owner owns 2+ boats and managing all listings is a part-time job.
  4. Owner is new to charter and wants paperwork / lista 6 / insurance coordinated.
  5. Boat is high-value (€500K+) and damage triage speed matters.

When DIY makes sense

  1. Owner lives near the marina and can be there for handover.
  2. Owner has charter experience and stable broker / insurer relationships.
  3. Boat is in a peak market with steady demand — listing optimisation matters less.
  4. Owner explicitly wants to keep more of the gross.

BoatHire24's hybrid approach

We offer self-listed (15% commission, you handle the boat) and full-service (18–22%, we handle everything) on the same platform. Many owners start self-listed, switch to full-service if life gets busy. Become a host to start.

faqs.q: What is boat charter management?
faqs.a: Full-service operation of a charter boat for the owner: listing, screening, paperwork, handover, damage triage, payouts. Typical commission 18–25% of gross. Distinct from self-listed peer-to-peer where the owner handles handover and paperwork themselves.

faqs.q: Should I use full-service or self-listed?
faqs.a: Full-service makes sense for absentee owners, busy professionals, multi-boat owners, or first-year-in-charter owners. Self-listed makes sense for owners who live near the boat, have charter experience, and want maximum take-home. Many owners switch from one to the other as life changes.

faqs.q: How much does charter management cost?
faqs.a: Typically 18–25% of gross commission for full-service from a peer-to-peer platform; 22–35% for traditional independent charter management companies. Self-listing on BoatHire24 is 15% flat with no upgrade fees.

faqs.q: Does charter management include insurance?
faqs.a: No — insurance is the owner's responsibility regardless of management model. Charter manager refers you to the right broker (Pantaenius / Una / Mapfre in Spain; BoatUS / Markel in the US) and handles claims liaison. You hold the policy and pay the annual premium.

faqs.q: Will charter management get me more bookings?
faqs.a: Usually 15–35% more than the average self-listed owner, because full-service operations write better listings, take better photos, manage calendar more aggressively, and respond to enquiries faster. Top-quartile self-listed owners often match full-service bookings without paying the premium.

faqs.q: What's the catch with charter management?
faqs.a: Higher commission, less owner control over individual booking decisions (most managers approve bookings within agreed parameters without checking with you), and dependency on the manager's quality. Switching costs are real — you'll need to rebuild calendar and rebuild trust if you change.

faqs.q: Can I list with both a charter manager and on BoatHire24 directly?
faqs.a: Yes — the charter manager handles their channel, you self-list on BoatHire24 as a second channel. iCal sync prevents double-booking. Most owners eventually consolidate to one approach but multi-channel during transition is common.

kw: how to rent out my boat
title: How to Rent Out Your Boat: 2026 Step-by-Step Guide | BoatHire24
h1: How to Rent Out Your Boat — Step-by-Step
metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to renting out your boat in 2026: legal setup, insurance, platform choice, pricing, listing copy. Real owner workflow.
intro: A practical step-by-step on how to rent out your boat in 2026 — covering legal structure, insurance, platform choice, pricing, and the listing copy that actually converts. Geared to first-year owners. Skip ahead to become a host if you already know the steps and just want to list.
bodyHtml:
Step 1: Confirm the boat is legally rentable

Every jurisdiction splits recreational from charter use. The label varies:

  • Spain: lista 7 (private) vs lista 6 (charter). Switch lista before first paid rental.
  • France: Plaisance (private) vs Commerce (charter). Declare commerciale use.
  • UK: Pleasure use vs Commercial — MCA Small Commercial Vessel code applies.
  • USA: Recreational vs commercial passenger transport. Coast Guard 6-pack or Uninspected Passenger Vessel rules apply over certain passenger counts.
  • Italy: Diporto vs Commerciale. Different ITS inspection.

Skipping the legal change voids your insurance and risks fines from local maritime authorities. Plan 4–8 weeks for the paperwork.

Step 2: Charter-grade insurance

Your pleasure-use policy excludes charter activity. Get a standalone annual charter policy from a marine specialist. Cost guide: $700–$9,000/year depending on boat size and value. See our detailed pages: US insurance and Spain / EU insurance.

Step 3: Pick your platform(s)

Best practice: list on 1 strong primary and 1 supplementary, sync calendars via iCal.

  • Mediterranean Europe: BoatHire24 (primary) + Click&Boat or Samboat (secondary). See Click&Boat alternative.
  • US: Boatsetter (primary in Florida / California) or GetMyBoat (primary on lakes) + BoatHire24 (secondary for international tourist traffic).
  • UK: Borrow A Boat (primary) + BoatHire24 (secondary). See Borrow A Boat alternative.
  • Australia: GetMyBoat (primary) + BoatHire24.

Step 4: Photos and video

The single biggest lever on bookings. Phone shots at midday vs golden-hour pro photos: 2–3× difference in click-through. Minimum: 10 photos (exterior cruising, interior cabin, helm, cockpit, swim platform, anchored). Strongly recommended: 60-second on-board video. Budget: €350–€800 for a professional shoot in Marbella / Cannes / Miami. Pays back in 2–4 bookings.

Step 5: Pricing strategy

  • Anchor on the local median. Look at 10 comparable boats on Click&Boat or Boatsetter — your low-season day rate should match the median.
  • Peak-weekend uplift: 25–40% above weekday rate for Friday-Sunday in July/August.
  • Multi-day discount: 10–15% off for 3+ day charters.
  • Early-bird: 10% off for bookings 60+ days out.
  • Last-minute: 10–20% off for bookings within 7 days (better than empty calendar).

Step 6: Listing copy that converts

What works: specific numbers (length, year, engine hours, fuel capacity), specific routes (named bays / coves / islands), specific inclusions (drinks, fuel, snacks named), clear honest descriptions of what isn't included. What doesn't: 'luxury', 'unforgettable', 'paradise', generic stock copy.

Step 7: First booking — handover

  1. Confirm renter ID and payment before handover.
  2. Walk through boat: controls, safety kit, MOB drill, return time.
  3. Photo check-in: timestamped photos of every visible exterior and interior surface.
  4. Hand over keys, give the renter the WhatsApp number for emergency.
  5. On return: photo check-out, damage walk-through, release deposit within 48 hours if clean.

Step 8: Review the season, iterate

End of season: count bookings, gross, net, charter days. Look at what worked and what didn't. Most owners earn 20–30% more in Year 2 than Year 1 with the same boat by tightening pricing, refreshing photos, and shortening response time.

Get started

List your boat on BoatHire24. Or read related: List your boat Marbella, Airbnb for boats explainer, Earnings calculator.

faqs.q: How do I start renting out my boat?
faqs.a: Five steps: (1) confirm the boat is legally rentable in your jurisdiction — usually a private/commercial registration switch; (2) buy charter-grade insurance from a marine specialist; (3) pick 1–2 platforms (BoatHire24, Boatsetter, Click&Boat, GetMyBoat, Borrow A Boat); (4) shoot pro photos and a video; (5) price against local median + 25–40% peak uplift.

faqs.q: Is it legal to rent out my boat?
faqs.a: Yes, with the correct registration. Every jurisdiction splits recreational from charter use (lista 7 vs 6 in Spain, Plaisance vs Commerce in France, MCA Small Commercial Vessel code in the UK, Coast Guard 6-pack rules in the US). You must switch before the first paid charter — typically 4–8 weeks of paperwork.

faqs.q: Do I need insurance to rent out my boat?
faqs.a: Yes, charter-grade. Pleasure-use policies exclude paid charter; one rental voids the year's cover. Annual charter premiums: $700–$1,400 for jet ski / small boat; $1,400–$3,500 for 10–14m yacht; $5,000–$9,000+ for 20m+ yacht. Standalone or platform-bundled.

faqs.q: How much should I charge?
faqs.a: Anchor on the local median for similar boats on Click&Boat / Boatsetter / BoatHire24. Add 25–40% for peak weekends (July–August Saturdays). Subtract 10–15% for multi-day. Most owners under-price by 10–20% in Year 1 and lose money relative to what the market would bear.

faqs.q: Which platform is best for me?
faqs.a: Mediterranean: BoatHire24 + Click&Boat. US Florida/California coast: Boatsetter + BoatHire24. US lakes: GetMyBoat. UK: Borrow A Boat. Australia: GetMyBoat. Most successful owners list on 2 platforms with iCal sync — 25–40% more bookings than single-platform.

faqs.q: How much time does it take to manage a listing?
faqs.a: Self-listed: 1–2 hrs/week in season, mostly handover days. Per charter: 1–3 hrs (handover, return inspection, message responses). Setup: 8–15 hrs (photos, copy, calendar, broker contact). Full-service charter management drops this to ~1 hour per month at the cost of higher commission.

faqs.q: What's the most common first-year mistake?
faqs.a: Under-pricing. Owners want to fill the calendar so they list 15–25% below market median. They get bookings but earn less than the market would pay, and attract a more deal-hunter renter pool. Better: price at median + accept slightly slower fill rate.

title: Pontoon Boat Rental Across the US | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Pontoon Boat Rental covers the full fleet around the United States: pontoon decks, water-slide pontoons, party platforms 8–14 guests. BoatHire24 lists priv
h1: Pontoon Boat Rental Across the US
intro: Pontoon Boat Rental covers the full fleet around the United States: pontoon decks, water-slide pontoons, party platforms 8–14 guests. BoatHire24 lists private and operator-managed boats with instant booking, transparent pricing, and licensed captain options. This page covers what's available, what to expect, day rates, and how to list your own boat if you keep one on the water nearby.
bodyHtml:
What's in the fleet around the United States

Demand around the United States centres on pontoon party rentals. Typical inventory across the platform:

  • covered pontoon, BBQ pontoon, water-slide pontoon
  • Captain-included day charters for groups of 4–14
  • Self-drive options for renters who hold a valid state boating safety card
  • Half-day (4h), full-day (8h), and sunset cruise (2h) rentals

Day rate ranges (2026)

Boat typeHalf-day (4h)Full day (8h)
Pontoon 22–26 ft (8–10 guests)$350–$550$550–$850
Tritoon / party pontoon (10–14 guests)$500–$750$800–$1,200
Ski / wakeboard boat$450–$700$750–$1,150
Sport yacht 30–40 ft$1,200–$2,000$2,000–$3,500

Captain typically $180–$280/day extra. Fuel pass-through. Cleaning $40–$80 per charter.

What's included

  • Pre-charter safety briefing at the dock
  • Life jackets, cooler, basic safety kit
  • Insurance for the rental period (operator-managed listings)
  • Captain if booked — fuel and fuel surcharge pass-through

Best time of year

Peak runs Memorial Day through Labor Day (late May to early September). Shoulder weekends in April and October are quieter and 20–30% cheaper. Book holiday weekends (4th of July, Memorial Day, Labor Day) 4–8 weeks ahead — top inventory sells out.

Own a boat on the United States? List it on BoatHire24

If you keep a boat near the United States, you can list it on BoatHire24 in under a week — free listing, flat 15% commission on booked days only, no monthly fee. We handle bookings, payments, and renter screening; you keep calendar control and approval over every booking. See our become-a-host page or compare us to Boatsetter and GetMyBoat for the owner-side comparison.

Nearby alternatives

  • Lake Havasu Pontoon Rental

Demand around Lake Powell, Utah / Arizona centres on houseboats, ski boats and pontoons. Typical inventory across the platform:

  • Aqua Patio houseboats, Bayliner ski boats
  • Captain-included day charters for groups of 4–14
  • Self-drive options for renters who hold a valid state boating safety card
  • Half-day (4h), full-day (8h), and sunset cruise (2h) rentals

Day rate ranges (2026)

Boat typeHalf-day (4h)Full day (8h)
Pontoon 22–26 ft (8–10 guests)$350–$550$550–$850
Tritoon / party pontoon (10–14 guests)$500–$750$800–$1,200
Ski / wakeboard boat$450–$700$750–$1,150
Sport yacht 30–40 ft$1,200–$2,000$2,000–$3,500
Day cruiser / deck boat$400–$600$650–$1,000

Captain typically $180–$280/day extra. Fuel pass-through. Cleaning $40–$80 per charter.

What's included

  • Pre-charter safety briefing at the dock
  • Life jackets, cooler, basic safety kit
  • Insurance for the rental period (operator-managed listings)
  • Captain if booked — fuel and fuel surcharge pass-through

Best time of year

Peak runs Memorial Day through Labor Day (late May to early September). Shoulder weekends in April and October are quieter and 20–30% cheaper. Book holiday weekends (4th of July, Memorial Day, Labor Day) 4–8 weeks ahead — top inventory sells out.

Own a boat on Lake Powell? List it on BoatHire24

If you keep a boat near Lake Powell, Utah / Arizona, you can list it on BoatHire24 in under a week — free listing, flat 15% commission on booked days only, no monthly fee. We handle bookings, payments, and renter screening; you keep calendar control and approval over every booking. See our become-a-host page or compare us to Boatsetter and GetMyBoat for the owner-side comparison.

Nearby alternatives

  • Lake Mead Boat Rentals
  • Lake Havasu Pontoon Rental
  • Lake Tahoe Boat Rentals

faqs.q: How much does it cost to rent a boat at Lake Powell?
faqs.a: Half-day rates start around $350 for a 22 ft pontoon and run to $2,000 for a 40 ft sport yacht. Full-day rates roughly 50–80% higher. Captain (if needed) is $180–$280/day on top. Fuel pass-through. Most Lake Powell renters spend $600–$1,200 for a half-day group outing.

faqs.q: Do I need a licence or boating card?
faqs.a: Most US states require a Boating Safety Course Certificate (boating card / NASBLA-approved) for self-drive operators born after a state-specific cutoff date. If you don't have one or don't want to drive, book with captain included.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faqs.a: Almost always yes — most owners explicitly allow BYO. Some larger party platforms include a basic cooler with ice; confirm at booking. Glass restrictions apply on most lake boats.

faqs.q: What if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: Operator decision based on forecast wind and waves. If the operator cancels for weather, you get a full refund or a free reschedule. Renter cancellation inside the cancellation window follows the standard policy on the listing.

faqs.q: How many people can a pontoon hold?
faqs.a: 22–24 ft pontoons typically take 8–10 guests; 26–28 ft tritoons take 12–14; large party platforms 16+. Coast Guard capacity is on the placard at the helm. Don't exceed it — both safety and insurance.

faqs.q: How early should I book?
faqs.a: Peak summer weekends (especially holiday weeks): 4–8 weeks ahead. Mid-week peak: 1–2 weeks. Shoulder season: a few days. Top boats sell out earliest.

faqs.q: Are there sunset cruise options at Lake Powell?
faqs.a: Yes — most listings offer a 2-hour sunset cruise option at a flat rate. Common on pontoons and sport yachts. Add catering or beverage package as an extra.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?

faqs.q: How many people can a pontoon hold?

title: Location de Bateau en France | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Location Bateau avec BoatHire24 : la marketplace européenne pour louer un bateau à France. Réservation directe, paiement sécurisé, pas de frais de service
intro: Location Bateau avec BoatHire24 : la marketplace européenne pour louer un bateau à France. Réservation directe, paiement sécurisé, pas de frais de service côté locataire. Cette page couvre la flotte disponible, les tarifs, les itinéraires classiques et comment lister votre propre bateau si vous en possédez un dans la région.
bodyHtml:
La flotte autour de France

Cadre : Toute la France. Bateaux disponibles : voiliers, bateaux à moteur, sans permis. Spots phares : lacs, fleuves, Côte d'Azur.

  • Demi-journée (4h), journée complète (8h), coucher de soleil (2h)
  • Avec ou sans skipper
  • Permis côtier ou fluvial requis selon le bateau (sans permis pour ≤ 6 CV)

Tarifs indicatifs 2026

TypeDemi-journéeJournée
Bateau sans permis (≤ 6 CV)€140–€220€220–€340
Semi-rigide 6–8 m€350–€550€550–€850
Bateau à moteur 8–10 m€600–€900€900–€1,400
Yacht 10–14 m€1,000–€1,800€1,800–€3,200
Voilier 35–45 ft€450–€750€750–€1,300

Skipper en supplément : €180–€280/jour. Carburant à part. Caution €500–€2,500 selon le bateau.

Ce qui est inclus

  • Briefing sécurité avant départ
  • Gilets de sauvetage, kit de sécurité
  • Assurance pendant la location (listings opérateurs)
  • Skipper si choisi

Meilleure période

Haute saison de mi-juin à fin août. Demande forte juillet-août, réservez 4–6 semaines à l'avance pour les meilleurs bateaux et les week-ends. Mai, juin et septembre offrent les meilleures conditions (mer plus calme, moins de monde, tarifs 15–25% plus bas).

Vous avez un bateau ? Listez-le sur BoatHire24

Propriétaire d'un bateau à France ? Listez-le gratuitement, commission flat 15% uniquement sur réservations confirmées. Voir devenir hôte ou comparer avec Click&Boat et Samboat.

À proximité

  • Location Bateau Marseille Vieux Port
  • Location Bateau Cannes Cote Azur
  • Location Bateau Sans Permis France

faqs.q: Combien coûte la location de bateau à France ?
faqs.a: De €140 pour une demi-journée sans permis à €3,200+ pour une journée sur un yacht 14 m. La majorité des locations groupes se situent entre €550 et €1,400 la journée complète.

faqs.q: Faut-il un permis bateau ?
faqs.a: Non pour les bateaux ≤ 6 CV (moteur, en eaux intérieures et côtières <2 milles). Oui pour tout bateau supérieur : permis côtier minimum. Sans permis = skipper inclus dans le tarif.

faqs.q: Quelle est la durée idéale de location ?
faqs.a: Demi-journée (4h) pour découvrir, journée (8h) pour un vrai itinéraire avec pause baignade et déjeuner. 2h coucher de soleil pour les couples et apéros.

faqs.q: Puis-je apporter ma nourriture et boissons ?
faqs.a: Oui presque toujours. Quelques opérateurs proposent un service traiteur en option. Confirmer à la réservation.

faqs.q: Que se passe-t-il en cas de mauvais temps ?
faqs.a: Décision du skipper ou de l'opérateur selon prévisions. Annulation côté loueur = remboursement intégral ou report sans frais.

faqs.q: Combien de personnes peut accueillir un bateau à France ?
faqs.a: Sans permis : 4–6 personnes. Semi-rigide 7m : 8–10. Bateau 9m : 10–12. Yacht 12m+ : 10–14. Capacité légale affichée à bord.

faqs.q: Comment annuler ma réservation ?
faqs.a: Politique standard : remboursement intégral 14 jours avant départ, 50% entre 7 et 14 jours, pas de remboursement à moins de 7 jours. Politique précise sur chaque annonce.

title: Norfolk Broads Boat Hire — Day & Week Cruisers | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Norfolk Broads Boat Hire through BoatHire24 covers Norfolk Broads: day cruisers, week boats with cabins, sailing cruisers. Self-drive day cruisers, holiday
h1: Norfolk Broads Boat Hire — Day & Week Cruisers
intro: Norfolk Broads Boat Hire through BoatHire24 covers Norfolk Broads: day cruisers, week boats with cabins, sailing cruisers. Self-drive day cruisers, holiday boats with cabins, sailing yachts — book directly from owners and operators with transparent pricing and instant confirmation.
bodyHtml:
What's on the water at Norfolk Broads

Setting: Norfolk Broads. Typical fleet: day cruisers, week boats with cabins, sailing cruisers. Highlights: How Hill, Horning, Salhouse Broad, Hickling.

  • Day boats (4h–8h) and week-long holiday cruisers
  • Self-drive — no licence required on Norfolk Broads; full handover briefing at hire base
  • Electric and diesel options
  • Weekly cruisers with 2–6 berth cabins, kitchen, WC, shower

Indicative rates (2026)

BoatDay hireWeek hire
4-person day boat (electric)£90–£160n/a
6-person day cruiser£120–£220n/a
4-berth week cruisern/a£1,100–£1,800
6-berth week cruisern/a£1,500–£2,800
8-berth premium cruisern/a£2,400–£4,200

Fuel pass-through (week cruisers). Damage deposit £150–£500.

What's included

  • Full safety briefing and boat handover at the hire base
  • Buoyancy aids, navigation chart, mooring kit
  • Bedding and basic galley equipment (week cruisers)
  • Insurance for the hire period

Best time of year

Peak: late May to early September. Week-long cruisers book up early for school holidays (last week of July to last week of August) — reserve 3–6 months ahead. May, June and September deliver the best weather-to-crowd balance and 20–30% cheaper rates.

Own a boat? List it on BoatHire24

Boat owners on Norfolk Broads can list directly on BoatHire24 — free listing, flat 15% commission, calendar control. See become-a-host or our Borrow A Boat alternative comparison.

Related

  • Wroxham Broads Boat Hire
  • Windermere Lake Boat Hire
  • Boat Hire Norfolk Broads Weekend

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to hire a boat on Norfolk Broads?
faqs.a: No. UK inland waters do not require a recreational boat licence for hirers. The hire base runs a full handover briefing covering controls, mooring, locks (where applicable), and safe operation. You're typically on the water within 30 minutes of arriving.

faqs.q: How much for a week on Norfolk Broads?
faqs.a: Mid-range 4-berth cruiser: £1,100–£1,800 in shoulder season, £1,800–£2,800 in summer school holidays. Premium 6–8 berth: £2,400–£4,200 peak. Includes boat, bedding, basic galley kit. Fuel pass-through.

faqs.q: Can we bring a dog?
faqs.a: Many boats allow dogs (£20–£50 supplement). Filter by 'pet-friendly' on the listing. Some restrictions on size and number — confirm at booking.

faqs.q: Do you provide bedding and towels?
faqs.a: Bedding (duvets, sheets, pillows) included on week cruisers. Towels usually not — bring your own or hire at the base (£3–£5 each).

faqs.q: What about mooring overnight?
faqs.a: Most hire boats can moor at marked moorings on the network (free or low fee). Marina overnight £15–£35 including electric hook-up. Plan a mix to balance scenery and convenience.

faqs.q: When should we book?
faqs.a: Summer school holidays (mid-July to late August): 3–6 months ahead. Shoulder season: 4–8 weeks. Off-peak (October–April): days to a couple of weeks ahead.

faqs.q: Are there short-day options?
faqs.a: Yes — day boats (4–8h hire) on most of the network. Smaller electric and outboard boats for 4–6 people, no overnight option but a great way to test the water before committing to a week.

intro: Wroxham Boat Hire through BoatHire24 covers Norfolk Broads: day cruisers, week boats with cabins, sailing cruisers. Self-drive day cruisers, holiday boats with cabins, sailing yachts — book directly from owners and operators with transparent pricing and instant confirmation.
bodyHtml:
What's on the water at Wroxham

Setting: Norfolk Broads. Typical fleet: day cruisers, week boats with cabins, sailing cruisers. Highlights: How Hill, Horning, Salhouse Broad, Hickling.

  • Day boats (4h–8h) and week-long holiday cruisers
  • Self-drive — no licence required on Wroxham; full handover briefing at hire base
  • Electric and diesel options
  • Weekly cruisers with 2–6 berth cabins, kitchen, WC, shower

Indicative rates (2026)

BoatDay hireWeek hire
4-person day boat (electric)£90–£160n/a
6-person day cruiser£120–£220n/a
4-berth week cruisern/a£1,100–£1,800
6-berth week cruisern/a£1,500–£2,800
8-berth premium cruisern/a£2,400–£4,200

Fuel pass-through (week cruisers). Damage deposit £150–£500.

What's included

  • Full safety briefing and boat handover at the hire base
  • Buoyancy aids, navigation chart, mooring kit
  • Bedding and basic galley equipment (week cruisers)
  • Insurance for the hire period

Best time of year

Peak: late May to early September. Week-long cruisers book up early for school holidays (last week of July to last week of August) — reserve 3–6 months ahead. May, June and September deliver the best weather-to-crowd balance and 20–30% cheaper rates.

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to hire a boat on Wroxham?
faqs.a: No. UK inland waters do not require a recreational boat licence for hirers. The hire base runs a full handover briefing covering controls, mooring, locks (where applicable), and safe operation. You're typically on the water within 30 minutes of arriving.

faqs.q: How much for a week on Wroxham?
faqs.a: Mid-range 4-berth cruiser: £1,100–£1,800 in shoulder season, £1,800–£2,800 in summer school holidays. Premium 6–8 berth: £2,400–£4,200 peak. Includes boat, bedding, basic galley kit. Fuel pass-through.

faqs.q: Can we bring a dog?
faqs.a: Many boats allow dogs (£20–£50 supplement). Filter by 'pet-friendly' on the listing. Some restrictions on size and number — confirm at booking.

faqs.q: Do you provide bedding and towels?
faqs.a: Bedding (duvets, sheets, pillows) included on week cruisers. Towels usually not — bring your own or hire at the base (£3–£5 each).

faqs.q: What about mooring overnight?
faqs.a: Most hire boats can moor at marked moorings on the network (free or low fee). Marina overnight £15–£35 including electric hook-up. Plan a mix to balance scenery and convenience.

faqs.q: When should we book?
faqs.a: Summer school holidays (mid-July to late August): 3–6 months ahead. Shoulder season: 4–8 weeks. Off-peak (October–April): days to a couple of weeks ahead.

faqs.q: Are there short-day options?
faqs.a: Yes — day boats (4–8h hire) on most of the network. Smaller electric and outboard boats for 4–6 people, no overnight option but a great way to test the water before committing to a week.

title: Sydney Harbour Boat Hire | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Boat Hire Sydney via BoatHire24 — book BBQ pontoons, party boats, sailing yachts and day cruisers around Sydney. Free booking, no service fees for renters,
intro: Boat Hire Sydney via BoatHire24 — book BBQ pontoons, party boats, sailing yachts and day cruisers around Sydney. Free booking, no service fees for renters, instant confirmation.
bodyHtml:
What's available around Sydney

Setting: Sydney Harbour. Typical fleet: BBQ pontoons, electric day boats, sailing yachts. Highlight spots: Opera House, Shark Island, Watsons Bay, Manly.

  • BBQ pontoons (most popular) — 10–14 guests, gas BBQ on board, no licence
  • Party boats with sound systems for groups 12–20
  • Self-drive day boats and sailing yachts
  • Sunset cruise (2h) and full-day (8h) options

Day rate ranges (2026)

BoatHalf-day (4h)Full day (8h)
BBQ pontoon 10-guestA$420–A$650A$650–A$1,000
BBQ pontoon 14-guestA$550–A$800A$850–A$1,250
Party boat 16+A$700–A$1,100A$1,100–A$1,800
Sailing yacht 38–45 ftA$850–A$1,400A$1,400–A$2,400
Motor yacht 35–45 ft (skipper)A$1,200–A$2,000A$2,000–A$3,500

Skipper A$280–A$420/day. Fuel pass-through. Cleaning A$80–A$150.

What's included

  • Safety briefing at the dock
  • Life jackets, sound system Bluetooth, basic galley kit
  • Insurance for the hire period
  • Skipper if booked

Best time of year

Peak: October to April (Southern Hemisphere summer). NSW/QLD: most BBQ pontoons run year-round; the water is warmest December to March. Book Saturday charters in December–February at least 4 weeks ahead.

Own a boat on Sydney? List it

List your boat free on BoatHire24, flat 15% commission, full calendar control. See become-a-host or our GetMyBoat alternative comparison for the AU/global picture.

Related

  • Boat Hire Melbourne Yarra
  • Boat Hire Gold Coast Broadwater
  • Noosa Boat Hire River

faqs.q: Do I need a boat licence on Sydney?
faqs.a: Most BBQ pontoons and party boats in NSW and QLD can be driven without a recreational skipper's ticket up to a certain HP limit, after a briefing at the hire base. Higher-power yachts require the relevant state RST or equivalent. Skippered hire bypasses the requirement entirely.

faqs.q: Can we BYO drinks and food?
faqs.a: Yes — almost universally on BBQ pontoons. Some operators charge a small corkage / cleaning fee. Glass restrictions apply on certain waterways. Confirm at booking.

faqs.q: How many people fit on a BBQ pontoon?
faqs.a: 10-guest is the standard small pontoon, 14-guest the popular mid-range, 16–20 for party boats. Capacity is on the placard — don't exceed it.

faqs.q: Is sunset cruising popular around Sydney?
faqs.a: Very. Most operators offer a 2-hour sunset slot at a flat rate. Particularly popular on Sydney Harbour, Gold Coast Broadwater, and Noosa River. Book the Saturday slot 2+ weeks ahead in peak summer.

faqs.q: What about jet ski and tube tow?
faqs.a: Many BBQ pontoons can tow donuts/tubes (extra hire) and some operators allow jet ski follow-on hire. Confirm operator-by-operator.

faqs.q: Cancellation policy?
faqs.a: Standard: full refund 14+ days out, 50% 7–14 days, no refund inside 7 days. Operator-cancelled (weather): full refund or free reschedule.

faqs.q: Weather backups?
faqs.a: Strong wind warnings (35 kn+) typically cancel pontoon hire. Operator decides on the day. You get refund or reschedule at no cost.

faqs.q: Do I need a boat licence on Mandurah?
faqs.a: Most BBQ pontoons and party boats in NSW and QLD can be driven without a recreational skipper's ticket up to a certain HP limit, after a briefing at the hire base. Higher-power yachts require the relevant state RST or equivalent. Skippered hire bypasses the requirement entirely.

faqs.q: Can we BYO drinks and food?
faqs.a: Yes — almost universally on BBQ pontoons. Some operators charge a small corkage / cleaning fee. Glass restrictions apply on certain waterways. Confirm at booking.

faqs.q: How many people fit on a BBQ pontoon?
faqs.a: 10-guest is the standard small pontoon, 14-guest the popular mid-range, 16–20 for party boats. Capacity is on the placard — don't exceed it.

faqs.q: Is sunset cruising popular around Mandurah?
faqs.a: Very. Most operators offer a 2-hour sunset slot at a flat rate. Particularly popular on Sydney Harbour, Gold Coast Broadwater, and Noosa River. Book the Saturday slot 2+ weeks ahead in peak summer.

faqs.q: What about jet ski and tube tow?
faqs.a: Many BBQ pontoons can tow donuts/tubes (extra hire) and some operators allow jet ski follow-on hire. Confirm operator-by-operator.

faqs.q: Cancellation policy?
faqs.a: Standard: full refund 14+ days out, 50% 7–14 days, no refund inside 7 days. Operator-cancelled (weather): full refund or free reschedule.

faqs.q: Weather backups?
faqs.a: Strong wind warnings (35 kn+) typically cancel pontoon hire. Operator decides on the day. You get refund or reschedule at no cost.

intro: Boat Rental Vancouver via BoatHire24 — book day boats, party pontoons, and sailing yachts around Vancouver. Direct booking, no renter service fees, instant confirmation.
bodyHtml:
What's on the water near Vancouver

Setting: Pacific coast. Typical fleet: boston whalers, day boats, sailing yachts. Highlights: Stanley Park, English Bay, Howe Sound.

  • Day boats (4h–8h)
  • BBQ pontoons and party platforms
  • Sailing yachts 30–45 ft
  • Sunset cruise (2h) and full-day options

Day rate ranges (2026)

BoatHalf-day (4h)Full day (8h)
Day boat 4-guestC$220–C$380C$380–C$650
Pontoon / deck boat 8-guestC$420–C$650C$650–C$1,000
Sailing yacht 35–45 ftC$700–C$1,150C$1,150–C$1,900
Motor yacht 40+ ft (skipper)C$1,200–C$2,000C$2,000–C$3,400

What's included

  • Life jackets, basic kit, Bluetooth audio

Best time of year

faqs.q: Do I need a licence for boat rental in Vancouver?
faqs.a: Canada requires a Pleasure Craft Operator Card (PCOC) for self-drive on most motorized vessels. Most operators check PCOC at the dock. Skippered hire bypasses the requirement.

faqs.q: Can we BYO food and drinks?
faqs.a: Yes, typically — confirm operator-by-operator. Open container rules on the water vary by province.

faqs.q: How many people fit on a pontoon?
faqs.a: 8-guest is standard, 12-guest mid-range, 14-guest party. Transport Canada capacity placard at the helm — don't exceed.

faqs.q: When does the season start in Vancouver?
faqs.a: Most operators open early May, peak runs late June through early September, season ends mid-October. Water temperature limits swimming on most lakes outside July/August.

faqs.q: Insurance?
faqs.a: Operator-managed listings carry hire-period coverage with deductible. Private peer-to-peer listings may require renter coverage — confirm at booking.

faqs.q: Cancellation policy?
faqs.a: Standard: full refund 14+ days, 50% 7–14, no refund inside 7. Weather cancellations refund in full.

faqs.q: Best alternative if weather turns?
faqs.a: Most operators allow free reschedule within the season if cancelled for weather. Indoor backups: museums (Vancouver Aquarium, ROM Toronto), restaurants on the waterfront — operator can advise.

title: Noleggio Barca Tropea — Calabria | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Noleggio Barca Tropea con BoatHire24: il marketplace per noleggiare una barca a Tropea. Prenotazione diretta dai proprietari e operatori, nessuna commissio
intro: Noleggio Barca Tropea con BoatHire24: il marketplace per noleggiare una barca a Tropea. Prenotazione diretta dai proprietari e operatori, nessuna commissione lato cliente, conferma immediata. In questa pagina: la flotta, i prezzi, gli itinerari classici e come listare la tua barca se sei proprietario.
bodyHtml:
La flotta a Tropea

Ambiente: costiera tirrenica. Tipologie disponibili: gozzi, barche a motore, semi-rigidi. Attrazioni: calette, grotte, snorkeling.

  • Mezza giornata (4h), giornata intera (8h), tramonto (2h)
  • Con o senza skipper
  • Patente nautica richiesta per natanti oltre 40 CV (sotto: non serve)

Tariffe indicative 2026

TipoMezza giornataGiornata
Gozzo 6m senza patente€140–€220€220–€340
Semi-rigido 7–8m€280–€450€450–€720
Barca a motore 9–11m€600–€950€950–€1,500
Yacht 12–14m€1,200–€2,000€2,000–€3,400
Barca a vela 38–45 ft€450–€750€750–€1,300

Skipper €180–€280/giorno. Carburante a parte. Cauzione €500–€2,500 secondo il natante.

Cosa è incluso

  • Briefing di sicurezza al molo
  • Salvagenti, kit di sicurezza
  • Assicurazione durante il noleggio (listing operatori)
  • Skipper se prenotato

Migliore periodo

Alta stagione da metà giugno a metà settembre. Picco di domanda 1–25 agosto: prenota con 4–6 settimane di anticipo. Maggio, giugno e fine settembre offrono mare più calmo, meno affollamento, tariffe ridotte del 20–30%.

Hai una barca? Listala su BoatHire24

Proprietari a Tropea: listing gratuito, commissione flat 15% solo su prenotazioni confermate. Diventa host o confronta con Click&Boat.

Nelle vicinanze

  • Noleggio Barca Capri Amalfi
  • Noleggio Barca Salerno Amalfi
  • Noleggio Barca Sardegna Costa

faqs.q: Quanto costa noleggiare una barca a Tropea?
faqs.a: Da €140 per mezza giornata su gozzo senza patente fino a €3,400+ per giornata intera su yacht 14m. Maggior parte dei gruppi spendono €450–€1,500 per giornata intera.

faqs.q: Serve la patente nautica?
faqs.a: Non per natanti fino a 40 CV in acque interne e a non più di 6 miglia dalla costa. Serve patente nautica entro 12 miglia (e per qualsiasi natante >40 CV). Senza patente = skipper incluso.

faqs.q: Posso portare cibo e bevande?
faqs.a: Sì, quasi sempre. Alcuni operatori offrono catering opzionale. Conferma alla prenotazione.

faqs.q: Cosa succede in caso di maltempo?
faqs.a: Decisione dello skipper o dell'operatore in base alle previsioni meteo. Annullamento operatore = rimborso integrale o rinvio gratuito.

faqs.q: Quante persone possono salire a bordo?
faqs.a: Gozzo 6m: 4–6 persone. Semi-rigido 7m: 8–10. Barca 9m: 10–12. Yacht 12m+: 10–14. Capacità legale indicata a bordo.

faqs.q: Quanto in anticipo prenotare?
faqs.a: Agosto: 6–8 settimane prima per i migliori bateau. Luglio: 3–4 settimane. Bassa stagione: pochi giorni.

faqs.q: Politica di cancellazione?
faqs.a: Standard: rimborso integrale 14+ giorni prima, 50% tra 7–14 giorni, nessun rimborso entro 7 giorni. Maltempo = rimborso integrale.

title: Boatsetter Alternative for Boat Owners (2026) — BoatHire24
h1: Boatsetter Alternative: BoatHire24 for Boat Owners
metaDescription: Looking for a Boatsetter alternative? BoatHire24 offers 15% commission (vs Boatsetter's 25–35% effective), EU-flagged boat coverage, and local marina support.
intro: If you're researching Boatsetter alternatives, you're likely an existing owner frustrated with the effective 25–35% take-rate after the insurance load, or an EU-based owner who learned the hard way that Boatsetter's bundled Geico/Markel insurance doesn't cover Spanish-, French- or Italian-flagged vessels. This page is the straight comparison: where BoatHire24 wins, where Boatsetter still leads, and how the actual money works out for a 12 m motor yacht earning €40,000 gross a year.
bodyHtml:
The honest comparison

BoatHire24Boatsetter
Owner commission15% flat~10% + insurance load → 25–35% effective
Renter fee0% (price shown = price paid)10–15% service fee
Bundled insuranceNo (you arrange — we connect brokers)Yes for US-flagged vessels (Geico/Markel)
EU-flagged vesselsFull coverageLimited / disputed
Local marina supportIn-person check-in/out (Med)Self-serve
Renter screeningWhatsApp voice call + group compositionLight, automated
Calendar synciCal — list on us + Boatsetter bothiCal — list on Boatsetter + us both
Strongest marketMediterranean (Spain, France, Italy, Greece, Croatia)US (Florida, California, Texas, Northeast)

The €40,000 yacht — how the math actually works

Take a 12 m motor yacht in Marbella, Cannes or Palma earning €40,000 gross across the season:

Line itemBoatHire24Boatsetter
Gross bookings€40,000€40,000
Platform commission−€6,000 (15%)−€4,000 (10%)
Insurance load (taken pre-payout)€0−€6,000–€10,000 (15–25%)
Your charter insurance (annual)−€2,500 (you pay broker)included in load
Net to owner€31,500€26,000–€30,000

You're €1,500–€5,500 better off per yacht per year with BoatHire24, and the gap widens as gross revenue grows because Boatsetter's load is a percentage, while your annual charter premium is fixed.

Where Boatsetter still wins

  • US-flagged boats in Florida, California, Texas: Boatsetter's home market. Bigger renter pool, brand familiarity, bundled US-friendly insurance. We're not the right pick.
  • Owners who don't want to source insurance: Boatsetter handles it; we send you to Pantaenius / Una / Mapfre.
  • Lake-only boats: Lake Havasu, Lake of the Ozarks, etc. — Boatsetter inventory is deep.

If your boat lives in Marbella, Cannes, Palma, Athens, or anywhere on the Med, we win. If it lives in Tampa, switch carefully.

Migrate from Boatsetter — how it works

  1. Send us your existing Boatsetter listing URL via WhatsApp. We replicate the boat profile in 24 hours.
  2. iCal sync — your existing Boatsetter calendar shows up on us, so no double-booking risk.
  3. List on both platforms for a season. Compare net take-home directly.
  4. Most owners we onboard from Boatsetter drop them within 2 seasons once the gap is obvious.

No listing fee, no contract lock-in. 30-day notice to drop us.

Should you list on both?

Yes, in most cases. The marketplaces don't fully overlap — Boatsetter brings US tourists searching English, BoatHire24 brings Spanish/French/German renters and Mediterranean walk-in. Cross-listing typically lifts bookings 25–40% in Year 1 vs single-platform.

faqs.q: Is BoatHire24 actually cheaper than Boatsetter for owners?
faqs.a: Yes once you account for Boatsetter's insurance load. Boatsetter's headline 10% commission grows to an effective 25–35% take-rate after the bundled Geico/Markel insurance comes out of payouts. BoatHire24 charges a flat 15% with no insurance markup — you arrange your own annual charter policy with a Spanish or French broker. Net to owner is typically €1,500–€5,500 higher per year on a 12 m yacht.

faqs.q: Does Boatsetter cover EU-flagged boats?
faqs.a: Limited. Boatsetter's bundled insurance via Geico Marine and Markel is geographically scoped — primarily US-flagged vessels. EU-flagged vessels (Spanish, French, Italian, Croatian) sit in a coverage grey zone that has caused payout disputes. EU owners should hold standalone annual charter insurance regardless of which platform they list on.

faqs.q: Can I list on both Boatsetter and BoatHire24?
faqs.a: Yes — we encourage it. iCal calendar sync prevents double-booking. Most cross-listed owners see 25–40% more bookings in Year 1 than single-platform, because the audiences barely overlap. Boatsetter brings US tourists; BoatHire24 brings European renters and Mediterranean walk-in.

faqs.q: What if my boat is in Florida or California?
faqs.a: Then Boatsetter is probably the better single-platform choice. US lake and coastal markets are Boatsetter's home turf. BoatHire24's strength is the Mediterranean (Spain, France, Italy, Greece, Croatia) and the EU broadly. Cross-listing still works but US-only owners get less marginal value from us.

faqs.q: How fast can I migrate from Boatsetter?
faqs.a: Listing live on BoatHire24 within 24–48 hours of sending your existing listing URL via WhatsApp. We replicate photos, copy, pricing, calendar. iCal sync engages immediately so dates booked on Boatsetter block out on us automatically. No double-booking risk during the transition.

faqs.q: Does BoatHire24 handle insurance disputes?
faqs.a: We don't underwrite, so we don't pay claims. We do coordinate with your insurer (Pantaenius / Una / Mapfre) — providing timestamped check-in/out photos, despacho records, renter screening transcripts. That paperwork accelerates settlement significantly versus what self-managed owners typically experience.

faqs.q: What boat sizes does BoatHire24 list?
faqs.a: Everything from license-free 5 m RIBs to crewed 30 m+ superyachts. Largest active fleet category: 8–18 m motor yachts. Sailing yachts and catamarans well-represented. Jet skis listed on a separate page. Above 24 m we usually broker rather than list publicly.

title: Click&Boat Alternative — Lower Commission, Same EU Reach | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Click&Boat alternative for boat owners: 15% commission, EU coverage, faster claims response, in-person Mediterranean check-in. Honest comparison.
intro: Click&Boat is Europe's biggest peer-to-peer boat rental marketplace — 22,200 monthly searches in France alone, ~280 boats listed around the Costa del Sol. If you're looking for a Click&Boat alternative, you're probably tired of the variable commission (15–22%), the optional-but-pushed insurance add-on, or the Paris-only owner support. This page is the straight comparison and the migration path.
bodyHtml:
How BoatHire24 compares to Click&Boat

BoatHire24Click&Boat
Owner commission15% flat15–22% (variable)
Renter service fee0%~6%
InsuranceYou arrange (we connect brokers)Optional add-on (renter pays)
Owner supportWhatsApp, Spanish + English + French, marina-basedEmail / portal, Paris HQ
Check-in/outIn-person at slip (Med)Self-serve
Renter screeningWhatsApp voice call, group compositionProfile + reviews
Strongest marketsCosta del Sol, Cannes, Palma, CroatiaFrance-wide, especially Med
Listing speed5–7 days with site visit2–4 days self-serve

Where Click&Boat is still strong

  • Top-of-funnel volume. 22,200 monthly brand searches in France, ~8,000 EU-wide. Click&Boat drives more raw enquiries than we do.
  • French inland waterways: Canal du Midi, Annecy, Gorges du Verdon — Click&Boat's home turf with deep inventory.
  • Self-serve owners who don't want a marina visit. Click&Boat lets you list in 2 days from your phone. We require a 20–60 minute walkthrough.

The realistic move: list on both. iCal sync, run a season, compare net take-home.

The commission gap on a €60,000-year yacht

LineBoatHire24Click&Boat (mid)
Gross€60,000€60,000
Commission−€9,000 (15%)−€10,800 (18%)
Insurance add-on (if used)n/a−€1,800 (renter-paid, suppresses bookings)
Net to owner€51,000€49,200

Small gap on commission alone, larger gap once you factor that owners who decline the Click&Boat insurance add-on see fewer bookings (cautious renters prefer insured listings). Net advantage to us: €1,800–€3,500/yr on a mid-sized yacht.

Migrate from Click&Boat — process

  1. Send your Click&Boat listing URL on WhatsApp. We pull photos and copy in 24h.
  2. Schedule a 30-minute marina walkthrough. We add 6–10 better photos and a 60-second video.
  3. iCal sync with your Click&Boat calendar — zero double-booking risk.
  4. List on both platforms. Most owners drop Click&Boat by Year 2 once the net-take-home gap is visible.

What about Samboat (Click&Boat's sister brand)?

Samboat was acquired by Click&Boat in 2018 and now runs as a parallel brand on the same underlying inventory. Listing on Click&Boat lists you on Samboat by default; the comparison above applies to both.

faqs.q: Is BoatHire24 the same as Click&Boat?
faqs.a: No. BoatHire24 is an independent Mediterranean-focused peer-to-peer marketplace. Click&Boat is the Paris-based market leader for European boat rentals. We share the same business model — listing private boats to renters with commission per booking — but differ on commission (15% flat vs 15–22% variable), insurance approach (you arrange vs platform add-on), and owner support (marina-based WhatsApp vs Paris email/portal).

faqs.q: What commission does Click&Boat charge owners in 2026?
faqs.a: Click&Boat's commission varies between 15% and 22% depending on the country, listing tier, and whether the booking comes via Click&Boat or its sister site Samboat. Renters also pay a ~6% service fee on top. BoatHire24 charges a flat 15% with no renter fee — the price the renter sees is the price they pay.

faqs.q: Can I list on both Click&Boat and BoatHire24?
faqs.a: Yes, and most of our owners do. iCal calendar sync prevents double-booking. Click&Boat brings French and German renter traffic; BoatHire24 brings Spanish-speaking, English-speaking and Mediterranean walk-in. Cross-listed boats typically see 30–45% more bookings than single-platform in Year 1.

faqs.q: Does Click&Boat's bundled insurance protect owners?
faqs.a: Click&Boat offers an optional insurance add-on paid by the renter, underwritten by a French insurer. It's not the same as an owner's annual charter policy — coverage scope is narrower, and EU charter regulations require you to hold standalone charter cover regardless. We recommend an annual policy via Pantaenius, Una, or Mapfre Náutica.

faqs.q: What's Click&Boat's strongest market?
faqs.a: France — especially the Côte d'Azur (Cannes, Nice, Antibes, Hyères), Brittany, and the French inland waterways (Canal du Midi, Annecy, Verdon). Also strong on the Spanish Med and the Balearics. Outside France, brand recognition drops; that's where independent platforms like BoatHire24 capture market share.

faqs.q: Is migrating from Click&Boat to BoatHire24 worth it?
faqs.a: For Mediterranean owners earning €25,000+ gross/year: yes, list on both. The commission and insurance-add-on gap nets you €1,500–€4,000 more per year per yacht. For French inland owners on Canal du Midi or Annecy: stay on Click&Boat as primary — that's their home turf — but adding BoatHire24 as a second listing costs nothing.

faqs.q: Will Click&Boat penalise me for cross-listing?
faqs.a: No. Click&Boat's terms of service allow multi-platform listing. Most established owners on Click&Boat are also on GetMyBoat, Samboat (same parent), Borrow A Boat or BoatHire24. The platforms compete for renters, not for exclusivity from owners.

title: GetMyBoat Alternative for Mediterranean Owners — BoatHire24
metaDescription: GetMyBoat alternative for Mediterranean boat owners — local marina support, faster claims, EU-flagged vessel coverage. Honest comparison.
intro: GetMyBoat is one of the lower-commission global boat rental marketplaces — 7–10% commission to owners, 7–10% to renters, free listings. The trade-off is light screening and a self-serve model. If you're researching a GetMyBoat alternative, you're probably either an EU owner where GetMyBoat's reach is thin, or a higher-value owner who wants more renter vetting before keys change hands.
bodyHtml:
BoatHire24 vs GetMyBoat — straight comparison

BoatHire24GetMyBoat
Owner commission15%7–10%
Renter service fee0%7–10%
Listing feeFreeFree
Renter screeningWhatsApp voice call, group composition disclosedLight — profile review only
In-person handoverYes (Mediterranean)No
Dispute speedSame-day (Med markets)3–7 days via portal
Strongest marketsMediterranean EuropeUS, Australia, Caribbean

The lower-commission trap

GetMyBoat's headline 7–10% commission is real, but the net economics often disappoint:

  • Light screening means more cancellations and disputes. Average GetMyBoat owner reports 18–25% cancellation rate; ours runs 8–12% because every renter goes through WhatsApp voice screening.
  • No in-person check-in means deposit disputes drag. When a renter claims the gelcoat damage was pre-existing, the platform sits in the middle for days. With us, a team member's at the slip with timestamped photos within 60 minutes.
  • Light insurance integration. GetMyBoat partners with Buoy / SafeCharter, but coverage is patchy outside the US.

Net on a €40,000-gross yacht: GetMyBoat owner takes home €36,000 gross but loses €1,800–€3,500 to cancellations and disputes; BoatHire24 owner takes €34,000 net with far fewer losses.

Where GetMyBoat is strong

  • US — Lake Tahoe, Lake Powell, Lake Mead, San Diego. Deep inventory.
  • Australia — Sydney, Gold Coast, Whitsundays.
  • Caribbean — BVI, USVI, Bahamas.
  • Owners who want pure self-serve with no marina visit.

Migrate from GetMyBoat

  1. Send GetMyBoat listing URL via WhatsApp.
  2. 30-minute marina walkthrough — we usually find 3–6 better photo angles.
  3. iCal sync, list on both, compare net over a season.

faqs.q: Is GetMyBoat cheaper than BoatHire24?
faqs.a: On the headline commission, yes — GetMyBoat takes 7–10% from owners vs our 15%. But you have to factor in cancellation rate (GetMyBoat owners report 18–25%, ours 8–12%) and dispute resolution speed. Net take-home after cancellations and damage disputes is typically similar to BoatHire24 in Mediterranean markets.

faqs.q: Does GetMyBoat cover Spanish or French-flagged boats?
faqs.a: GetMyBoat lists EU-flagged vessels but insurance integration via Buoy / SafeCharter is patchy outside the US. EU owners need standalone annual charter insurance regardless of platform. We recommend Pantaenius (Med-wide), Una (Spain), or Pantaenius France.

faqs.q: Can I list on GetMyBoat and BoatHire24 together?
faqs.a: Yes. iCal sync engages automatically; no double-booking. We see this combination working especially well for Mediterranean owners who want global English-speaking traffic (GetMyBoat strength) plus marina-side support (BoatHire24 strength).

faqs.q: How does renter screening compare?
faqs.a: GetMyBoat relies on platform profile + prior reviews. BoatHire24 runs a WhatsApp voice call with every renter before owner approval — confirming group composition, prior charter experience, and plans for the day. About 12% of enquiries get politely declined at our screening stage. GetMyBoat declines fewer, accepts more cancellations.

faqs.q: What's GetMyBoat's strongest geography?
faqs.a: US lake markets (Tahoe, Powell, Mead, Havasu), US coastal cities (San Diego, Miami, Tampa), Australia (Sydney, Gold Coast, Whitsundays), and the Caribbean (BVI, USVI, Bahamas). Mediterranean inventory is thinner than Click&Boat or BoatHire24.

faqs.q: Is GetMyBoat free to list?
faqs.a: Yes — both platforms (GetMyBoat and BoatHire24) have free listings with no monthly fee. Both take commission only when a booking confirms. Differences are in commission rate, renter screening, dispute handling, and geographical reach.

faqs.q: Does BoatHire24 handle damage claims?
faqs.a: We coordinate. Insurance underwriting and payouts are with your annual charter insurer (Pantaenius / Una / Mapfre). What we do: timestamped check-in/out photos, despacho records, renter screening transcripts, on-marina inspection within 60 minutes of a reported incident. That documentation typically accelerates settlement by 2–4 weeks vs self-managed cases.

title: Samboat Alternative — Independent Mediterranean Platform | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Samboat alternative: BoatHire24 is independent (not Click&Boat-owned), 15% commission, marina-based support, full EU coverage.
intro: Samboat was a French peer-to-peer boat rental pioneer until Click&Boat acquired it in 2018. Today Samboat runs as a parallel brand on Click&Boat's underlying inventory — same commission structure (15–22%), same Paris support, same approach. If you're looking for a Samboat alternative, the real question is whether you want an independent platform or another Click&Boat-family listing. This page covers the difference.
bodyHtml:
Samboat is now Click&Boat

Since the 2018 acquisition, Samboat and Click&Boat share back-end inventory, commission policy, and operational support. Listing on one effectively lists on both. The brand differences are cosmetic — UI styling, marketing focus (Samboat skews sailing yachts, Click&Boat all boats), and country preference (Samboat slightly stronger in francophone West Africa and the French Antilles).

If you want a genuinely independent peer-to-peer marketplace as a counterweight to the Click&Boat/Samboat duopoly, the choices are: BoatHire24 (independent, Med-focused), Boatsetter (US-focused), GetMyBoat (US/AU/Caribbean), or Borrow A Boat (UK-focused).

BoatHire24 vs Samboat — comparison

BoatHire24Samboat (= Click&Boat)
OwnershipIndependentClick&Boat subsidiary
Commission15% flat15–22% variable
Renter fee0%~6%
Listing setup5–7 days, marina walkthrough2–4 days self-serve
Support languageEN, ES, FR (WhatsApp)FR, EN (email/portal)
Sailing yacht focusStrong (catamarans, monohulls)Samboat brand skews here
Marina-based handoverYesNo

Why owners cross-list instead of switching

Samboat's underlying inventory is the same as Click&Boat's, so dropping Samboat doesn't drop Click&Boat traffic — those renters find your listing via the parent brand anyway. The practical owner move: keep your Samboat/Click&Boat listing, add BoatHire24 as a second, sync calendars. Same renter audience, different acquisition channels.

Migrating to BoatHire24 alongside Samboat

  1. Send WhatsApp message with Samboat listing URL.
  2. 30-minute marina walkthrough. Photos, video, copy.
  3. iCal sync — Samboat bookings block our calendar and vice-versa.
  4. Live on both within a week. Compare net take-home across a season.

faqs.q: Is Samboat still independent from Click&Boat?
faqs.a: No. Click&Boat acquired Samboat in 2018. The two brands now share back-end inventory and commission structure. Listing on Samboat lists you on Click&Boat by default. Brand differences are marketing-only — Samboat skews sailing yachts, Click&Boat is generalist.

faqs.q: Should I list on Samboat AND BoatHire24?
faqs.a: Yes for most Mediterranean owners.
